vocabulary.definition: the allocation of items to groups according to types and the sorting and grouping of items/objects according to their attributes/characteristics

sub_topics.title: Classification
competences: Classify items according to their attributes

learner_steps: Go round the school surrounding
List what they see/touch
Play games with stones, sticks, lid tops in games like 'iciyenga' etc
Answer oral questions on mentioning stones used in games of draft, 'iciyenga' etc
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Exploring the environment

teacher_steps: Show the items to the Learners according to size quantity
learner_steps: Identify list of items in groups according to size and quantity
Differentiate items according to quantity and size
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Identifying of various objects according to given criteria such as quantity and size. Given a list of items e.g. (5 litres of cooking oil, 2.5 litres of cooking oil,1kg of salt, 2kg of mealie meal, 500g of sugar and 500g of salt)

teacher_steps: Present various fruits to the Learners
Show the sizes and numbers of the fruits
learner_steps: Match sizes and number of the given fruits
Match the sizes and number of fruits given correctly
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Matching familiar objects according to given criteria such as size and number

teacher_steps: Give each chair to a given child
Explain to learners to get only one pencil from 12 pencils displayed on the table in relation to their names
Line up learners to get each cup according his/her name
Ask questions on how many books, pencils, crayons etc. if the class has only 12 learners
learner_steps: Get one chair each
Get one pencil each according to their names
Get one cup each according to their names
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Matching objects in one-to-one correspondence e.g. a chair for each child

teacher_steps: Display different bottle tops on the table
Allow all the learners to see/touch the objects on the display
Allow the learners to sort out the bottle tops according to size and shape
learner_steps: Group objects according to their sizes and shapes
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Sorting objects according to given criteria such as size and shape

teacher_steps: Display the assorted bottle tops, lid tops on the table
Explain how a given bottle top/lid fits a given bottle
Divide the learners into groups and allow them to sort the bottle tops according to the given bottles
learner_steps: Sort out the given bottle tops to the given bottles individually
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Sorting objects using puzzles and games (shoe size, height)

teacher_steps: Display the kitchen utensils on the table
Allow learners to come forward to see/touch the utensils
Explain the functions of the given utensils to the learners
learner_steps: Group the given objects according to their functions and similarities
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Grouping objects according to the given criteria such as functions and similarities

teacher_steps: Arrange assorted items at the table like sticks, cloth, rulers, pencils, balls, shoes etc
Explain to the learners the objects displayed on the table that they are of different shapes, height, length and size
learner_steps: Arrange the assorted objects according to their length, shapes, height and size
List the assorted objects according to their shapes, length, size and height individually
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Ordering objects according to given criteria such as length, height, size and shape

teacher_steps: Find out from pupils which daily routine activities they know
Discuss the daily routine activities (Brushing of teeth, combing of hair, eating breakfast, going to school, washing/bathing)
learner_steps: Identify the daily routine activities correctly
Demonstrate how these daily routine activities are done
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Ordering daily routine activities

teacher_steps: Let the learners mention the days of the week
Jumble the days of week and ask them to identify the first and last day of the week
Divide learners into groups and then sing the song 'number one, number 2 where are you?' While others mention the days of the week
learner_steps: Mention the days of the week
Sing the song 'number one, number 2 where are you?' While others mention the days of the week
Pair up and revise the numbers and the days of the week
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Telling number stories

sub_topics.short_notes: Classification is the allocation of items to groups according to types. It is important in one's life as it instills a sense of recognition of characteristics of items/objects. It helps us to distinguish between two or more items/objects.

questions: Classification of objects according to their attributes through observation
Question and answer on classification
Group work on classification
Demonstration of classification
Puzzles and games on classification

vocabulary.definition: parts of the body which are visibly seen. They include hair, eyes, nose, teeth, skin, hands and legs
the practice of keeping your body clean and healthy by washing and grooming yourself
the physical substance of the human organism, composed of living cells and extra cellular materials and organized into tissues and organs

sub_topics.title: External parts of the body
competences: Recognize and name the external parts of the human body

teacher_steps: Present a toy in the class representing a human body
Tell the learners to touch all the parts of that toy while teacher names those parts
learner_steps: Mention the parts of the body individually
Sing a song naming the parts of the body
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Identifying the external parts of the human body (Head, eyes, nose, mouth, arms, hands and feet) through games and songs

teacher_steps: Allow the learners to stand up and mention their parts of the body individually
Ask the learners what each part of the body does
Explain the functions of each part of the body
learner_steps: Sing a song mentioning the function of each part of the body
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Relating external parts of the human body to their functions through songs and activities such as eyes for seeing, hands for touching/holding, legs for walking

sub_topics.short_notes: External parts of the body are parts of the body which are visibly seen. They include hair, eyes, nose, teeth, skin, hands and legs.

questions: Identification of external parts of human body
Relate these parts to their functions

sub_topics.title: Personal Hygiene

teacher_steps: Demonstrate to the learners on how to wash the hands
Put learners into pairs to practice
learner_steps: Follow what the teacher demonstrated
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Demonstrating proper hand washing using songs or rhymes

teacher_steps: Display materials used for brushing on the table
Demonstrate on how to use these materials step by step
learner_steps: Do the actual brushing of the teeth
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Demonstrating how to brush teeth using appropriate materials

teacher_steps: Explain to the learners the toilet facilities
Demonstrate how to use it (Flushing and covering)
learner_steps: Demonstrate/practice how to flush and cover the toilet hole in pairs
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Practicing using toilet facilities (Flushing and covering the toilet after use, opening and covering the drop hole)

sub_topics.short_notes: Personal hygiene is the practice of keeping your body clean and healthy by washing and grooming yourself. This includes washing your hands and practicing oral hygiene.

questions: How to brush their teeth
Practice using toilet facilities

vocabulary.definition: any substance consumed by an organism for nutritional support. Food is usually of plant, animal, or fungal origin and contains essential nutrients such as carbohydrates, fats, proteins, vitamins or minerals
the practice of handling, preparing and storing food to prevent contamination and food borne illness
foods that are rich in nutrients
foods that are high in calories and low in nutrients
a science of human nutrition or science of food, the nutrients and other substances therein, their action, interaction and balance in relation to health and diseases

sub_topics.title: Food

teacher_steps: Define fruits, vegetables, grains, proteins and dairy
Divide the class into groups, ask the pupils to identify and name various types of food
learner_steps: Identify the various types of food
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Identifying and naming various types of food (fruits, vegetables, grains, and proteins, dairy)

sub_topics.short_notes: Food is any substance consumed by an organism for nutritional support. Food is usually of plant, animal, or fungal origin and contains essential nutrients such as carbohydrates, fats, proteins, vitamins or minerals.

questions: Identification and naming of various types of food

sub_topics.title: Food Hygiene

teacher_steps: Explain the importance of food hygiene
Display the foodstuffs available and allow learners to come forward to the display table
Demonstrate on how foodstuffs should be handled
learner_steps: Practice on how to keep food hygienically
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Demonstrate food hygienic practices of handling foods, such as washing hands, fruits, vegetables and covering food

teacher_steps: Demonstrate to learners on how to remove the utensils from the table
Demonstrate on how to clean/wash of the utensils
learner_steps: Practice on how to clean/wash the utensils
Wipe and clean the surfaces
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Washing of utensils after eating

teacher_steps: Explain to the learners about the leftover food
Show the leftover food to learners
Demonstrate how to warm the food
Ask learners on how to warm leftover food orally
learner_steps: Answer orally on how to warm leftover food
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Practising warming leftover food before eating

sub_topics.short_notes: Food hygiene is the practice of handling, preparing and storing food to prevent contamination and food borne illness.

questions: How to keep food hygienically
Find ways of not making food (leftover food) to go bad

sub_topics.title: Healthy and Unhealthy Foods

teacher_steps: Ask learners about healthy and unhealthy foods
Discuss with learners on healthy and unhealthy foods
learner_steps: Mention unhealthy and healthy foods orally in groups
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Identifying healthy and unhealthy food choices

teacher_steps: Explain differences between healthy and unhealthy foods
Identify healthy and unhealthy foods with learners
learner_steps: Identify the unhealthy and healthy foods orally
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Differentiating between healthy and unhealthy food choices (Vegetables being healthier choices compared to sugary snacks)

sub_topics.short_notes: Healthy foods are rich in nutrients, while unhealthy foods are high in calories and low in nutrients.

questions: Identify healthy and unhealthy foods
Show the differences between the two types of foodstuffs

vocabulary.definition: a way to represent numbers using symbols or digits
mathematical objects to count, measure, and label. They can be written as numerals like '1' or as words, like 'one'

sub_topics.title: Numbers

teacher_steps: Explain to the learners that they will count numbers 1-10 and real objects 1-5
Demonstrate to the learners on how to count from 1-10 orally, using a song
Demonstrate to learners on counting numbers 1-5 using stones
learner_steps: Count in pairs using stones, 1-5 and orally using songs
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Counting orally numbers 1-10, counting concrete or real objects 1-5

teacher_steps: Revise activity one by giving a child a bundle of sticks to get five from these
Demonstrate writing numbers using concrete objects from 1-5
Ask pupils to practice writing numbers 1-5
learner_steps: Count numbers individually 1-5
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Identifying numbers 1-5 using objects

teacher_steps: Ask individual learners to identify written numbers 1-5 using objects
Ask learners to count written numbers 1-5 using objects
Heap different objects in 1,2,3,4,5 and ask learners to name numbers according to the heap of numbers
Ask pupils to identify written numbers and count numbers 1-5 using objects
learner_steps: Identify written numbers 1-5 using objects
Count written numbers 1-5 using objects
Name numbers according to the heap of objects
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Identify written numbers 1-5 using objects

teacher_steps: Give objects to the learners according to the number of object each learner has (1-5)
Call out numbers 1-5, and ask learners having the number mentioned to stand and name the number
Ask pupils to read the written numbers (1-5) using objects
Ask learners to name numbers 1-5 using objects
learner_steps: Stand when their number is called and name the number
Read the written numbers (1-5) using objects
Name numbers 1-5 using objects
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Naming numbers 1-5 using objects

teacher_steps: Explain to the learners how the game is played
Demonstrate to the learners how the game is played, eg. Five holes are made and the teacher places a stone in the third hole, then the learner hops to the third hole where the stone is etc
Divide learners into pairs to practice the game
Divide into groups and do a competition on hopscotch
learner_steps: Practice the hopscotch game in pairs
Compete in groups on hopscotch
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Recognizing the value of numbers 1-5 using real objects through hopscotch game

teacher_steps: Explain to the learners how matching familiar objects according to their value (1-5)
Demonstrate by matching the numbers 1 to 1 stone, 2 to 2 books, 3, 3 pencils etc
learner_steps: Match familiar objects to 1-5 to bottles, pens, books etc
Do individual work on the same work
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Matching familiar objects according to their value (1-5)

teacher_steps: Explain to the learners on how to write (emboss)
Discuss with learners on how to write numeral sign as dot 3 and 456
learner_steps: Emboss numeral sign and immediately dot 1, dot 1 2, dots 1 3 etc
Write (emboss, 1-5) correctly
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Embossed numbers 1-5

sub_topics.short_notes: Numbers are mathematical objects to count, measure, and label. They can be written as numerals like '1' or as words, like 'one'.

questions: How to recognize numbers from 1-10
Emboss numbers from 1-5 correctly

vocabulary.definition: mathematical operation that involve combining or removing number. They are represented by plus sign (+) and (-)
the process of calculating the total of two or more numbers or amounts
the operation or process of finding the difference between two numbers or quantity

sub_topics.title: Addition

teacher_steps: Explain to the learners the process of adding numbers from 1-5 using concrete objects
Demonstrate to the learners using concrete object in showing 1 plus 1, which makes two, 1 plus 2, makes 3 etc
Divide learners into groups to add objects to the sum of five
learner_steps: Use concrete objects in adding to the sum of five individually
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Adding concrete objects up to the sum of 5

sub_topics.short_notes: Addition is the process of calculating the total of two or more numbers or amounts.

questions: How to add concrete things up to 5
Use the mathematical operation of addition correctly

sub_topics.title: Subtraction

teacher_steps: Explain the process of subtraction using concrete objects
Demonstrate the process using concrete objects eg. 3-2, 4-1, and 3-1 etc
learner_steps: Use concrete objects in the subtraction up to the difference of 4
Subtract up to the difference of 4 using concrete objects individually
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Subtracting concrete objects up to the difference of 4

sub_topics.short_notes: Subtraction is the operation or process of finding the difference between two numbers or quantity.

questions: Subtraction up to the difference of 4 using concrete materials

vocabulary.definition: in mathematics define the outline or the boundary of an object, the shapes can be classified into different types based on their properties

sub_topics.title: Shapes

teacher_steps: Introduce the lesson on making lines using real objects
Demonstrate using the material available to make lines
learner_steps: Practice making lines using the given materials
Make lines using the real objects given correctly individually
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Making lines using real objects

teacher_steps: Introduce the lesson by giving out cut outs
Demonstrate with the given material on how to make shapes
Divide learners into groups to make different shapes
learner_steps: Construct shapes using puzzles and other items individually
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Construct shapes using puzzle and other items

teacher_steps: Explain the meaning of a 2 dimensional shape (a shape with a length and width that can be drawn on the flat surface)
Give examples; shapes, like triangles, circle, rectangle etc
Create some 2 dimensional shapes on the flat surface like a paper etc with learners
learner_steps: Create at least 2 shapes of 2 dimensional correctly individually
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Creating 2 dimensional shape diagrams

teacher_steps: Explain the meaning to the learners that 3 D means solid objects that have length, width and height
Discuss names of such shapes like cones, sphere, cylinder etc with learners
Demonstrate on how to make such solid shapes like cuboids and cone
learner_steps: Make at least 2 3 dimensional shape correctly
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Making 3 dimensional shapes

teacher_steps: Revise activity 3 and 4 with the learners
Discuss 2 dimensional and 3 dimensional shapes with learners
learner_steps: Compare the shapes discussed of 2 dimensional and 3 dimensional
Discuss orally on the comparison of the shapes
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Comparing shapes according to size

teacher_steps: Explain on how to trace shapes
Demonstrate on how to trace the shapes, mould and do some cut outs
Divide learners into groups and tell them to trace using cut outs
learner_steps: Trace the shapes using the cut outs individually
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Tracing shapes

sub_topics.short_notes: Shapes in mathematics define the outline or the boundary of an object, the shapes can be classified into different types based on their properties.

questions: Creating shapes of 2 dimensional
Creating shapes of 3 dimensional
Tracing the shapes correctly

vocabulary.definition: the process of quantifying the size of an object or event. It is used to compare objects or events to a standard reference
to ascertain the size, amount or degree of (something) by using an instrument or device marked in standard units

sub_topics.title: Measure
competences: Tell time by activities of the day

teacher_steps: Explain to the learners the activities of the day (daily routine)
Discuss with the learners on what they do when they wake up
List/mention the daily routine for their days with learners
learner_steps: Tell time of the activities of their day individually
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Telling time by activities of the day

teacher_steps: Explain to the learners that the days of the week are not the same (e.g. Monday)
Sing the song on the days of the week (Sunday, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday) with learners
Discuss on what days they go to school and not (which days are more important than others) in groups
Revise on the days of the week with learners
learner_steps: Sing the song on the days of the week
Discuss in groups on what days they go to school
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Telling the day of the week

teacher_steps: Explain to the learners that non-standard unit of measurement is units of measurement that are not typically used. For example, pencil, toothpick etc
Demonstrate measuring length and width of different objects using non-standard units
Divide learners into pairs and ask them to practice measuring length and width of different objects using non-standard units
Revise on measuring lengths and widths of different objects using non-standard units with learners
learner_steps: Practice measuring length and width of different objects using non-standard units in pairs
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Measuring lengths and widths of different objects using non-standard units

teacher_steps: Explain to learners on comparing lengths and widths of different objects
Divide learners into groups and ask them to compare lengths and widths of different objects
Revise on comparing lengths and widths of different objects with learners
learner_steps: Compare lengths and widths of different objects individually
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Compare lengths and widths of different objects

sub_topics.short_notes: Measurement is the process of quantifying the size of an object or event. It is used to compare objects or events to a standard reference.

questions: Telling time by using activities of the day
Telling the days of the week
Measuring lengths and widths of different objects using non-standard units
Comparing lengths and widths of different objects
